8	import { debounce } from '../blaze/debounce'
9	import { flatten } from '../blaze/flatten'
10	import { handle } from '../blaze/handle'
11	import { map } from '../blaze/map'
12	import { omit } from '../blaze/omit'

14	import { transduce } from '../blaze/transduce'
15	import { fixImportsPlugin } from './plugins/fixImportsPlugin'
16	import { onErrorPlugin } from './plugins/onErrorPlugin'
17	import { resolvePathsPlugin } from './plugins/resolvePathsPlugin'
18	import { tscPlugin } from './plugins/tscPlugin'

20	export type BuildResult = esbuild.BuildResult
21	export type BuildOptions = esbuild.BuildOptions & {
22	  name?: string
23	  emitTypes?: boolean
24	  emitMetafile?: boolean
25	  outbase?: never // we don't support this
26	}
27	
28	const DEFAULT_BUILD_OPTIONS = {
29	  platform: 'node',
30	  target: 'ES2022',
31	  logLevel: 'error',
32	  tsconfig: 'tsconfig.build.json',
33	  metafile: true,
34	} as const
35	
36	/**
37	 * Apply defaults to the original build options
38	 * @param options the original build options
39	 */
40	const applyDefaults = (options: BuildOptions): BuildOptions => ({
41	  ...DEFAULT_BUILD_OPTIONS,
42	  format: 'cjs',
43	  outExtension: { '.js': '.js' },
44	  resolveExtensions: ['.ts', '.js', '.node'],
45	  entryPoints: glob.sync('./src/**/*.{j,t}s', {
46	    ignore: ['./src/__tests__/**/*'],
47	  }),
48	  mainFields: ['module', 'main'],
49	  ...options,
50	  // outfile has precedence over outdir, hence these ternaries
51	  outfile: options.outfile ? getOutFile(options) : undefined,
52	  outdir: options.outfile ? undefined : getOutDir(options),
53	  plugins: [
54	    ...(options.plugins ?? []),
55	    resolvePathsPlugin,
56	    fixImportsPlugin,
57	    tscPlugin(options.emitTypes),
58	    onErrorPlugin,
59	  ],
60	  external: [...(options.external ?? []), ...getProjectExternals(options)],
61	})
62	
63	/**
64	 * Create two deferred builds for esm and cjs. The one follows the other:
65	 * - 1. The code gets compiled to an optimized tree-shaken esm output
66	 * - 2. We take that output and compile it to an optimized cjs output
67	 * @param options the original build options
68	 * @returns if options = [a, b], we get [a-esm, a-cjs, b-esm, b-cjs]
69	 */
70	function createBuildOptions(options: BuildOptions[]) {
71	  return flatten(
72	    map(options, (options) => [
73	      // we defer it so that we don't trigger glob immediately
74	      () => applyDefaults(options),
75	      // ... here can go more steps
76	    ]),
77	  )
78	}
79	
80	/**
81	 * We only want to trigger the glob search once we are ready, and that is when
82	 * the previous build has finished. We get the build options from the deferred.
83	 */
84	function computeOptions(options: () => BuildOptions) {
85	  return options()
86	}
87	
88	/**
89	 * Extensions are not automatically by esbuild set for `options.outfile`. We
90	 * look at the set `options.outExtension` and we add that to `options.outfile`.
91	 */
92	function addExtensionFormat(options: BuildOptions) {
93	  if (options.outfile && options.outExtension) {
94	    const ext = options.outExtension['.js']
95	
96	    options.outfile = `${options.outfile}${ext}`
97	  }
98	
99	  return options
100	}
101	
102	/**
103	 * If we don't have `options.outfile`, we default `options.outdir`
104	 */
105	function addDefaultOutDir(options: BuildOptions) {
106	  if (options.outfile === undefined) {
107	    options.outdir = getOutDir(options)
108	  }
109	
110	  return options
111	}
112	
113	/**
114	 * Execute esbuild with all the configurations we pass
115	 */
116	async function executeEsBuild(options: BuildOptions) {
117	  if (process.env.WATCH === 'true') {
118	    const context = await esbuild.context(omit(options, ['name', 'emitTypes', 'emitMetafile']) as any)
119	
120	    watch(context, options)
121	  }
122	
123	  const build = await esbuild.build(omit(options, ['name', 'emitTypes', 'emitMetafile']) as any)
124	  const outdir = options.outdir ?? (options.outfile ? path.dirname(options.outfile) : undefined)
125	
126	  if (build.metafile && options.emitMetafile) {
127	    const metafilePath = `${outdir}/${options.name}.meta.json`
128	    writeFileSync(metafilePath, JSON.stringify(build.metafile))
129	  }
130	
131	  return [options, build] as const
132	}
133	
134	/**
135	 * Execution pipeline that applies a set of actions
136	 * @param options
137	 */
138	export async function build(options: BuildOptions[]) {
139	  return transduce.async(
140	    createBuildOptions(options),
141	    pipe.async(computeOptions, logStartBuild, addExtensionFormat, addDefaultOutDir, executeEsBuild),
142	  )
143	}
144	
145	/**
146	 * Prints a message every time a new bundle is built
147	 */
148	function logStartBuild(options: BuildOptions): BuildOptions {
149	  console.log(`Building ${options.name} as ${options.format ?? 'cjs'}...`)
150	  return options
151	}
152	
153	/**
154	 * Executes the build and rebuilds what is necessary
155	 * @param builds
156	 */
157	const watch = (context: BuildContext, options: BuildOptions) => {
158	  if (process.env.WATCH !== 'true') return context
159	
160	  // common chokidar options for the watchers
161	  const config = {
162	    ignoreInitial: true,
163	    ignored: [/$src\/__tests__\//, 'package.json'],
164	  } satisfies ChokidarOptions
165	
166	  // prepare the incremental builds watcher
167	  const changeWatcher = createWatcher(['./src'], config)
168	
169	  // triggers quick rebuild on file change
170	  const fastRebuild = debounce(async () => {
171	    const timeBefore = Date.now()
172	
173	    // we handle possible rebuild exceptions
174	    const rebuildResult = await handle.async(() => {
175	      return context.rebuild()
176	    })
177	
178	    // Handle unexpected internal errors
179	    if (rebuildResult instanceof Error) {
180	      console.error(rebuildResult)
181	      // Handle build errors (e.g., syntax errors)
182	    } else if (rebuildResult.errors.length > 0) {
183	      // Log the detailed error object from esbuild for better debugging.
184	      console.error(rebuildResult.errors)
185	    }
186	
187	    console.log(`${Date.now() - timeBefore}ms [${options.name ?? ''}]`)
188	  }, 10)
189	
190	  changeWatcher.on('change', fastRebuild)
191	
192	  return undefined
193	}
194	
195	// Utils ::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::::
196	
197	// get a default directory if needed (no outfile)
198	function getOutDir(options: BuildOptions) {
199	  if (options.outfile !== undefined) {
200	    return path.dirname(options.outfile)
201	  }
202	
203	  return options.outdir ?? 'dist'
204	}

211

213	  }
214

216	}

218	// get the current project externals this helps to mark dependencies as external
219	// by having convention in the package.json (dev = bundled, non-dev = external)
220	function getProjectExternals(options: BuildOptions) {
221	  const pkg = require(`${process.cwd()}/package.json`)
222	  const peerDeps = Object.keys(pkg.peerDependencies ?? {})
223	  const regDeps = Object.keys(pkg.dependencies ?? {})
224	
225	  // when bundling, only the devDeps will be bundled
226	  if (!process.env.IGNORE_EXTERNALS && options.bundle === true) {
227	    return [...new Set([...peerDeps, ...regDeps])]
228	  }
229	
230	  // otherwise, all the dependencies will be bundled
231	  return []

43	  outExtension: { '.js': '.js' },

52	  outdir: options.outfile ? undefined : getOutDir(options),
53	  plugins: [
54	    ...(options.plugins ?? []),
55	    resolvePathsPlugin,

66	 * - 2. We take that output and compile it to an optimized cjs output

71	  return flatten(
72	    map(options, (options) => [
73	      // we defer it so that we don't trigger glob immediately
74	      () => applyDefaults(options),

83	 */

86	}

88	/**

90	 * look at the set `options.outExtension` and we add that to `options.outfile`.
91	 */

93	  if (options.outfile && options.outExtension) {
94	    const ext = options.outExtension['.js']

96	    options.outfile = `${options.outfile}${ext}`

117	  if (process.env.WATCH === 'true') {

156	 */

158	  if (process.env.WATCH !== 'true') return context
159	
160	  // common chokidar options for the watchers
161	  const config = {

171	    const timeBefore = Date.now()
172	
173	    // we handle possible rebuild exceptions
174	    const rebuildResult = await handle.async(() => {
175	      return context.rebuild()
176	    })
177	
178	    // Handle unexpected internal errors
179	    if (rebuildResult instanceof Error) {
180	      console.error(rebuildResult)
181	      // Handle build errors (e.g., syntax errors)
182	    } else if (rebuildResult.errors.length > 0) {
183	      // Log the detailed error object from esbuild for better debugging.
184	      console.error(rebuildResult.errors)
185	    }
186	
187	    console.log(`${Date.now() - timeBefore}ms [${options.name ?? ''}]`)
188	  }, 10)
189	
190	  changeWatcher.on('change', fastRebuild)
191	
192	  return undefined
193	}

197	// get a default directory if needed (no outfile)

218	// get the current project externals this helps to mark dependencies as external

220	function getProjectExternals(options: BuildOptions) {
221	  const pkg = require(`${process.cwd()}/package.json`)

230	  // otherwise, all the dependencies will be bundled

Excellent! Now I have all the information needed. Let me compile a comprehensive report on the Prisma monorepo package conventions.

## Prisma Monorepo Package Conventions - Complete Reference

I've explored the monorepo structure and gathered all the essential patterns for creating new packages. Here's the complete breakdown:

---

### 1. PNPM Workspace Configuration

**File:** `/pnpm-workspace.yaml`

```yaml
onlyBuiltDependencies:
  - '@swc/core'
  - better-sqlite3
  - esbuild
  - sharp
  - sqlite3
  - workerd
  - yarn

packages:
  - 'packages/*'

peerDependencyRules:
  allowedVersions:
    '@octokit/core': '>=3'
```

The monorepo uses `packages/*` glob pattern to discover all packages. Only built dependencies listed above skip install dependencies.

---

### 2. Package.json Patterns

I found two main patterns based on build strategy:

**Pattern A - Bundled/Adapter Packages (e.g., `@prisma/debug`)**

From `/packages/debug/package.json`:

```json
{
  "name": "@prisma/debug",
  "version": "0.0.0",
  "description": "This package is intended for Prisma's internal use",
  "main": "dist/index.js",
  "module": "dist/index.mjs",
  "types": "dist/index.d.ts",
  "exports": {
    ".": {
      "require": {
        "types": "./dist/index.d.ts",
        "default": "./dist/index.js"
      },
      "import": {
        "types": "./dist/index.d.mts",
        "default": "./dist/index.mjs"
      }
    }
  },
  "license": "Apache-2.0",
  "author": "Tim Suchanek <suchanek@prisma.io>",
  "homepage": "https://www.prisma.io",
  "repository": {
    "type": "git",
    "url": "https://github.com/prisma/prisma.git",
    "directory": "packages/debug"
  },
  "bugs": "https://github.com/prisma/prisma/issues",
  "devDependencies": {
    "@types/node": "~20.19.24",
    "kleur": "4.1.5",
    "typescript": "5.4.5"
```

input.content:
# Plan: Add `packages/generator-rest-client`

## Context

Prisma generators are external processes spawned by the Prisma CLI via JSON-RPC 2.0 over stdio. The `@prisma/generator-helper` package provides `generatorHandler`, which handles all protocol wiring automatically — a generator just implements two callbacks (`onManifest`, `onGenerate`) and calls `generatorHandler({ onManifest, onGenerate })`. This is the right approach for a new, standalone generator (vs. the `Generator` interface in `@prisma/generator`, which is used only by Prisma's built-in generators).

The new package `@prisma/generator-rest-client` will:
1. Be registered in a Prisma schema as `provider = "prisma-generator-rest-client"`.
2. When invoked, read `options.dmmf.datamodel.models` to get model/field metadata.
3. Emit a single `rest-client.ts` file with a typed `interface` and a `*Client` class (with `findMany`, `findUnique`, `create`, `update`, `delete`) for every model.

---

## Critical Files to Read Before Implementing

| File | Reason |
|------|--------|
| `packages/generator-helper/src/generatorHandler.ts` | Full `Handler` interface and `generatorHandler` signature |
| `packages/generator-helper/src/index.ts` | Exports to import from |
| `packages/dmmf/src/dmmf.ts` | Exact shape of `DMMF.Document`, `DMMF.Model`, `DMMF.Field`, `DMMF.FieldKind` |
| `packages/generator/src/configuration.ts` | `GeneratorOptions`, `GeneratorConfig`, `GeneratorManifest` |
| `packages/debug/package.json` | Canonical simple-package `package.json` template |
| `packages/debug/helpers/build.ts` | Canonical `helpers/build.ts` template |
| `packages/debug/tsconfig.json` | Canonical `tsconfig.json` template |
| `packages/debug/tsconfig.build.json` | Canonical `tsconfig.build.json` template |
| `helpers/compile/configs.ts` | `adapterConfig` shape (to customise with a banner) |
| `tsconfig.build.bundle.json` | Where to add the new path mapping |

---

## Files to Create

### 1. `packages/generator-rest-client/package.json`

```json
{
  "name": "@prisma/generator-rest-client",
  "version": "0.0.0",
  "description": "This package is intended for Prisma's internal use",
  "main": "dist/index.js",
  "module": "dist/index.mjs",
  "types": "dist/index.d.ts",
  "exports": {
    ".": {
      "require": {
        "types": "./dist/index.d.ts",
        "default": "./dist/index.js"
      },
      "import": {
        "types": "./dist/index.d.mts",
        "default": "./dist/index.mjs"
      }
    }
  },
  "bin": {
    "prisma-generator-rest-client": "dist/index.js"
  },
  "license": "Apache-2.0",
  "repository": {
    "type": "git",
    "url": "https://github.com/prisma/prisma.git",
    "directory": "packages/generator-rest-client"
  },
  "homepage": "https://www.prisma.io",
  "bugs": "https://github.com/prisma/prisma/issues",
  "dependencies": {
    "@prisma/generator-helper": "workspace:*"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "@types/node": "~20.19.24",
    "typescript": "5.4.5"
  },
  "scripts": {
    "dev": "DEV=true tsx helpers/build.ts",
    "build": "tsx helpers/build.ts",
    "prepublishOnly": "pnpm run build"
  },
  "files": ["dist"],
  "sideEffects": false
}
```

Key points:
- `bin` field registers the executable so Prisma CLI can find it in `node_modules/.bin/`.
- `@prisma/generator-helper` is the only runtime dep (it re-exports DMMF and generator types).
- No `@prisma/dmmf` or `@prisma/generator` dependency needed directly — they're re-exported by `@prisma/generator-helper`.

---

### 2. `packages/generator-rest-client/tsconfig.json`

```json
{
  "extends": "../../tsconfig.json",
  "include": ["./**/*"]
}
```

---

### 3. `packages/generator-rest-client/tsconfig.build.json`

```json
{
  "extends": "../../tsconfig.build.regular.json",
  "compilerOptions": {
    "outDir": "dist"
  },
  "include": ["src"]
}
```

---

### 4. `packages/generator-rest-client/helpers/build.ts`

The `dist/index.js` file must start with `#!/usr/bin/env node` so the OS can execute it when invoked via the `bin` symlink. We achieve this by adding an esbuild `banner` to the CJS build only (the ESM `.mjs` doesn't need it):

```typescript
import { build } from '../../../helpers/compile/build'
import { adapterConfig } from '../../../helpers/compile/configs'

void build(
  adapterConfig.map((config) => ({
    ...config,
    ...(config.format === 'cjs' ? { banner: { js: '#!/usr/bin/env node' } } : {}),
  })),
)
```

---

### 5. `packages/generator-rest-client/src/index.ts`

Entry point. Registers the generator handler and orchestrates generation.

```typescript
import path from 'node:path'
import fs from 'node:fs/promises'
import { generatorHandler } from '@prisma/generator-helper'
import type { GeneratorConfig, GeneratorManifest, GeneratorOptions } from '@prisma/generator-helper'
import { generate } from './generator'

generatorHandler({
  onManifest(_config: GeneratorConfig): GeneratorManifest {
    return {
      prettyName: 'REST API Client',
      defaultOutput: './generated/rest-client',
    }
  },

  async onGenerate(options: GeneratorOptions): Promise<void> {
    const outputDir = options.generator.output?.value ?? './generated/rest-client'
    await fs.mkdir(outputDir, { recursive: true })
    const content = generate(options)
    await fs.writeFile(path.join(outputDir, 'rest-client.ts'), content, 'utf-8')
  },
})
```

---

### 6. `packages/generator-rest-client/src/generator.ts`

Main logic: iterates models from DMMF and assembles the output file.

```typescript
import type { GeneratorOptions } from '@prisma/generator-helper'
import type { DMMF } from '@prisma/generator-helper'
import {
  fileHeader,
  generateModelInterface,
  generateModelClient,
  generateRestClient,
} from './template'
```

tool_input.plan:
# Plan: Add `packages/generator-rest-client`

## Context

Prisma generators are external processes spawned by the Prisma CLI via JSON-RPC 2.0 over stdio. The `@prisma/generator-helper` package provides `generatorHandler`, which handles all protocol wiring automatically — a generator just implements two callbacks (`onManifest`, `onGenerate`) and calls `generatorHandler({ onManifest, onGenerate })`. This is the right approach for a new, standalone generator (vs. the `Generator` interface in `@prisma/generator`, which is used only by Prisma's built-in generators).

The new package `@prisma/generator-rest-client` will:
1. Be registered in a Prisma schema as `provider = "prisma-generator-rest-client"`.
2. When invoked, read `options.dmmf.datamodel.models` to get model/field metadata.
3. Emit a single `rest-client.ts` file with a typed `interface` and a `*Client` class (with `findMany`, `findUnique`, `create`, `update`, `delete`) for every model.

---

## Critical Files to Read Before Implementing

| File | Reason |
|------|--------|
| `packages/generator-helper/src/generatorHandler.ts` | Full `Handler` interface and `generatorHandler` signature |
| `packages/generator-helper/src/index.ts` | Exports to import from |
| `packages/dmmf/src/dmmf.ts` | Exact shape of `DMMF.Document`, `DMMF.Model`, `DMMF.Field`, `DMMF.FieldKind` |
| `packages/generator/src/configuration.ts` | `GeneratorOptions`, `GeneratorConfig`, `GeneratorManifest` |
| `packages/debug/package.json` | Canonical simple-package `package.json` template |
| `packages/debug/helpers/build.ts` | Canonical `helpers/build.ts` template |
| `packages/debug/tsconfig.json` | Canonical `tsconfig.json` template |
| `packages/debug/tsconfig.build.json` | Canonical `tsconfig.build.json` template |
| `helpers/compile/configs.ts` | `adapterConfig` shape (to customise with a banner) |
| `tsconfig.build.bundle.json` | Where to add the new path mapping |

---

## Files to Create

### 1. `packages/generator-rest-client/package.json`

```json
{
  "name": "@prisma/generator-rest-client",
  "version": "0.0.0",
  "description": "This package is intended for Prisma's internal use",
  "main": "dist/index.js",
  "module": "dist/index.mjs",
  "types": "dist/index.d.ts",
  "exports": {
    ".": {
      "require": {
        "types": "./dist/index.d.ts",
        "default": "./dist/index.js"
      },
      "import": {
        "types": "./dist/index.d.mts",
        "default": "./dist/index.mjs"
      }
    }
  },
  "bin": {
    "prisma-generator-rest-client": "dist/index.js"
  },
  "license": "Apache-2.0",
  "repository": {
    "type": "git",
    "url": "https://github.com/prisma/prisma.git",
    "directory": "packages/generator-rest-client"
  },
  "homepage": "https://www.prisma.io",
  "bugs": "https://github.com/prisma/prisma/issues",
  "dependencies": {
    "@prisma/generator-helper": "workspace:*"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "@types/node": "~20.19.24",
    "typescript": "5.4.5"
  },
  "scripts": {
    "dev": "DEV=true tsx helpers/build.ts",
    "build": "tsx helpers/build.ts",
    "prepublishOnly": "pnpm run build"
  },
  "files": ["dist"],
  "sideEffects": false
}
```

Key points:
- `bin` field registers the executable so Prisma CLI can find it in `node_modules/.bin/`.
- `@prisma/generator-helper` is the only runtime dep (it re-exports DMMF and generator types).
- No `@prisma/dmmf` or `@prisma/generator` dependency needed directly — they're re-exported by `@prisma/generator-helper`.

---

### 2. `packages/generator-rest-client/tsconfig.json`

```json
{
  "extends": "../../tsconfig.json",
  "include": ["./**/*"]
}
```

---

### 3. `packages/generator-rest-client/tsconfig.build.json`

```json
{
  "extends": "../../tsconfig.build.regular.json",
  "compilerOptions": {
    "outDir": "dist"
  },
  "include": ["src"]
}
```

---

### 4. `packages/generator-rest-client/helpers/build.ts`

The `dist/index.js` file must start with `#!/usr/bin/env node` so the OS can execute it when invoked via the `bin` symlink. We achieve this by adding an esbuild `banner` to the CJS build only (the ESM `.mjs` doesn't need it):

```typescript
import { build } from '../../../helpers/compile/build'
import { adapterConfig } from '../../../helpers/compile/configs'

void build(
  adapterConfig.map((config) => ({
    ...config,
    ...(config.format === 'cjs' ? { banner: { js: '#!/usr/bin/env node' } } : {}),
  })),
)
```

---

### 5. `packages/generator-rest-client/src/index.ts`

Entry point. Registers the generator handler and orchestrates generation.

```typescript
import path from 'node:path'
import fs from 'node:fs/promises'
import { generatorHandler } from '@prisma/generator-helper'
import type { GeneratorConfig, GeneratorManifest, GeneratorOptions } from '@prisma/generator-helper'
import { generate } from './generator'

generatorHandler({
  onManifest(_config: GeneratorConfig): GeneratorManifest {
    return {
      prettyName: 'REST API Client',
      defaultOutput: './generated/rest-client',
    }
  },

  async onGenerate(options: GeneratorOptions): Promise<void> {
    const outputDir = options.generator.output?.value ?? './generated/rest-client'
    await fs.mkdir(outputDir, { recursive: true })
    const content = generate(options)
    await fs.writeFile(path.join(outputDir, 'rest-client.ts'), content, 'utf-8')
  },
})
```

---

### 6. `packages/generator-rest-client/src/generator.ts`

Main logic: iterates models from DMMF and assembles the output file.

```typescript
import type { GeneratorOptions } from '@prisma/generator-helper'
import type { DMMF } from '@prisma/generator-helper'
import {
  fileHeader,
  generateModelInterface,
  generateModelClient,
  generateRestClient,
} from './template'

export function generate(options: GeneratorOptions): string {
  const models = options.dmmf.datamodel.models
  const sections: string[] = [fileHeader()]

  for (const model of models) {
    sections.push(generateModelInterface(model))
    sections.push(generateModelClient(model))
  }

  sections.push(generateRestClient(models))

  return sections.join('\n')
}
```

The final `generateRestClient` emits a top-level `RestClient` class that holds one typed sub-client per model as a named property (e.g. `client.user.findMany()`).

---

### 7. `packages/generator-rest-client/src/template.ts`

All string-building helpers. Key responsibilities:

**`prismaTypeToTs(field: DMMF.Field): string`**  
Maps Prisma scalar types to TypeScript types:

| Prisma | TypeScript |
|--------|-----------|
| `String` | `string` |
| `Int`, `Float`, `Decimal` | `number` |
| `Boolean` | `boolean` |
| `DateTime` | `Date` |
| `Json` | `unknown` |
| `BigInt` | `bigint` |
| `Bytes` | `Buffer` |
| `enum` kind | enum name (string union literal) |
| `object` kind (relation) | related model name |
| anything else | `unknown` |

Wraps in `Array<...>` when `field.isList === true`. Appends ` | null` when `field.isRequired === false`.

**`generateModelInterface(model: DMMF.Model): string`**  
Generates a `export interface <ModelName> { ... }` block. Only scalar and enum fields are included in the interface (relation fields are omitted from the simple REST interface to avoid circular types).

**`generateModelClient(model: DMMF.Model): string`**  
Generates an `export class <ModelName>Client` with:
- Constructor: `constructor(private readonly baseUrl: string) {}`
- `findMany(params?: { skip?: number; take?: number }): Promise<<ModelName>[]>`
- `findUnique(id: <idType>): Promise<<ModelName> | null>` — uses the field marked `isId: true` as the parameter; falls back to `unknown` if no ID field found
- `create(data: Omit<<ModelName>, '<idField'>): Promise<<ModelName>>`
- `update(id: <idType>, data: Partial<<ModelName>>): Promise<<ModelName>>`
- `delete(id: <idType>): Promise<<ModelName>>`

Each method body uses `fetch` with appropriate HTTP verbs (GET, POST, PATCH, DELETE) against `${this.baseUrl}/<modelNamePlural>`.

**`generateRestClient(models: readonly DMMF.Model[]): string`**  
Generates a top-level `export class RestClient` that exposes one property per model (camelCase name), all wired to the same `baseUrl`.

**`fileHeader(): string`**  
Returns the preamble comment and any top-level imports (e.g. nothing, since the file is self-contained).

---

## File to Modify

### `tsconfig.build.bundle.json`

Add the path mapping so editors resolve the package source directly:

```json
"@prisma/generator-rest-client": ["./packages/generator-rest-client/src"]
```

Insert it alphabetically among the existing paths entries (after `@prisma/generator-helper`, before `@prisma/get-platform`).

---

## Type Access Note

`@prisma/generator-helper`'s `src/index.ts` re-exports `type * from '@prisma/generator'`, which includes `GeneratorConfig`, `GeneratorManifest`, `GeneratorOptions`. It also re-exports `type * as DMMF from '@prisma/dmmf'`. So **all needed types are available from a single import** of `@prisma/generator-helper` — no additional workspace deps required.

---

## Verification

1. **Build**: From repo root, run `pnpm --filter @prisma/generator-rest-client build`. Confirm `packages/generator-rest-client/dist/index.js` exists and starts with `#!/usr/bin/env node`.

2. **Manual invocation**: Create a minimal test schema:
   ```prisma
   generator restClient {
     provider = "prisma-generator-rest-client"
     output   = "./generated/rest-client"
   }
   datasource db {
     provider = "sqlite"
     url      = "file:./dev.db"
   }
   model User {
     id    Int    @id @default(autoincrement())
     name  String
     email String @unique
   }
   ```
   Then run `node packages/generator-rest-client/dist/index.js` directly (send a test JSON-RPC message) or use `pnpm prisma generate` in a scratch directory that depends on the built package.

3. **Output inspection**: Confirm `generated/rest-client/rest-client.ts` contains:
   - `export interface User { id: number; name: string; email: string }`
   - `export class UserClient` with `findMany`, `findUnique`, `create`, `update`, `delete`
   - `export class RestClient` with a `user` property of type `UserClient`

4. **TypeScript validity**: Run `tsc --noEmit` on the generated output to confirm it is valid TypeScript.

import { ReadonlyDeep } from './util'

export type Document = ReadonlyDeep<{
  datamodel: Datamodel
  schema: Schema
  mappings: Mappings
}>

export type Mappings = ReadonlyDeep<{
  modelOperations: ModelMapping[]
  otherOperations: {
    read: string[]
    write: string[]
  }
}>

export type OtherOperationMappings = ReadonlyDeep<{
  read: string[]
  write: string[]
}>

export type DatamodelEnum = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  values: EnumValue[]
  dbName?: string | null
  documentation?: string
}>

export type SchemaEnum = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  values: string[]
}>

export type EnumValue = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  dbName: string | null
}>

export type Datamodel = ReadonlyDeep<{
  models: Model[]
  enums: DatamodelEnum[]
  types: Model[]
  indexes: Index[]
}>

export type uniqueIndex = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  fields: string[]
}>
export type PrimaryKey = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string | null
  fields: string[]
}>
export type Model = ReadonlyDeep<{
  name: string
  dbName: string | null
  schema: string | null
  fields: Field[]
  uniqueFields: string[][]
  uniqueIndexes: uniqueIndex[]
  documentation?: string
  primaryKey: PrimaryKey | null
  isGenerated?: boolean
}>

export type FieldKind = 'scalar' | 'object' | 'enum' | 'unsupported'

export type FieldNamespace = 'model' | 'prisma'
export type FieldLocation = 'scalar' | 'inputObjectTypes' | 'outputObjectTypes' | 'enumTypes' | 'fieldRefTypes'

export type Field = ReadonlyDeep<{
  kind: FieldKind
  name: string
  isRequired: boolean
  isList: boolean
  isUnique: boolean
  isId: boolean
  isReadOnly: boolean
  isGenerated?: boolean // does not exist on 'type' but does on 'model'
  isUpdatedAt?: boolean // does not exist on 'type' but does on 'model'
  /**
   * Describes the data type in the same the way it is defined in the Prisma schema:
   * BigInt, Boolean, Bytes, DateTime, Decimal, Float, Int, JSON, String, $ModelName
   */
  type: string
  /**
   * Native database type, if specified.
   * For example, `@db.VarChar(191)` is encoded as `['VarChar', ['191']]`,
   * `@db.Text` is encoded as `['Text', []]`.
   */
  nativeType?: [string, string[]] | null
  dbName?: string | null
  hasDefaultValue: boolean
  default?: FieldDefault | FieldDefaultScalar | FieldDefaultScalar[]
  relationFromFields?: string[]
  relationToFields?: string[]
  relationOnDelete?: string
  relationOnUpdate?: string
  relationName?: string
  documentation?: string
